server.h(3) Library Functions Manual server.h(3)

NAME

server.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct acds_client_data_t
Per-client connection data. struct migration_context_t
In-memory host migration context. struct acds_server_t
Discovery server state.

Functions


asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *server, const acds_config_t *config)
Initialize discovery server. asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *server)
Run discovery server main loop. void acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t *server)
Shutdown discovery server. void * acds_client_handler (void *arg)
Per-client connection handler (thread entry point).

Function Documentation

void * acds_client_handler (void * arg)

Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP packets from a connected client. Handles crypto handshake, then dispatches packets to session/signaling handlers.

Parameters

arg Pointer to client connection context

Returns

NULL (thread exit value)

Definition at line 896 of file src/discovery-service/server.c.

896                                      {
897   tcp_client_context_t *ctx = (tcp_client_context_t *)arg;
898   if (!ctx) {
899     log_error("Client handler: NULL context");
900     return NULL;
901   }
902 
903   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
904   socket_t client_socket = ctx->client_socket;
905 
906   // Get client IP for logging
907   char client_ip[INET6_ADDRSTRLEN] = {0};
908   tcp_client_context_get_ip(ctx, client_ip, sizeof(client_ip));
909 
910   log_info("Client handler started for %s", client_ip);
911 
912   // Register client in TCP server registry with allocated client data
913   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
914   if (!client_data) {
915     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to allocate client data");
916     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
917     return NULL;
918   }
919   memset(client_data, 0, sizeof(*client_data));
920   client_data->joined_session = false;
921   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
922 
923   // Initialize crypto handshake context with client IP name
924   char crypto_name[96];
925   SAFE_SNPRINTF(crypto_name, sizeof(crypto_name), "crypto_discovery_client_%s", client_ip);
926   asciichat_error_t handshake_result = crypto_handshake_init(crypto_name, &client_data->handshake_ctx, true);
927   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
928     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto handshake for client %s", client_ip);
929     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
930     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
931     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
932     return NULL;
933   }
934 
935   // Set server identity keys for handshake
936   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
937   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
938   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.key, server->identity_public, 32);
939   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.key.ed25519, server->identity_secret, 64);
940 
941   if (tcp_server_add_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
942     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
943     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to register client in registry");
944     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
945     return NULL;
946   }
947 
948   log_debug("Client %s registered (socket=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, client_socket,
949             tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
950 
951   // Perform crypto handshake (three-step process)
952   log_debug("Performing crypto handshake with client %s", client_ip);
953 
954   // Step 0: Send crypto parameters
955   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters_socket(&client_data->handshake_ctx, client_socket);
956   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
957     log_warn("Crypto parameters send failed for client %s", client_ip);
958     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
959     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
960     return NULL;
961   }
962 
963   // Step 1: Start handshake (send server key, receive client key)
964   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_start_socket(&client_data->handshake_ctx, client_socket);
965   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
966     log_warn("Crypto handshake start failed for client %s", client_ip);
967     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
968     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
969     return NULL;
970   }
971 
972   // Step 2: Authentication challenge (if required)
973   handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge_socket(&client_data->handshake_ctx, client_socket);
974   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
975     log_warn("Crypto handshake auth challenge failed for client %s", client_ip);
976     tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
977     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
978     return NULL;
979   }
980 
981   // Step 3: Complete handshake (verify and finalize) - skip if already complete
982   if (client_data->handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
983     handshake_result = crypto_handshake_server_complete_socket(&client_data->handshake_ctx, client_socket);
984     if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
985       log_warn("Crypto handshake complete failed for client %s", client_ip);
986       tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
987       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
988       return NULL;
989     }
990   } else {
991     log_debug("Crypto handshake already complete from auth_challenge step for client %s", client_ip);
992   }
993 
994   client_data->handshake_complete = true;
995   log_info("Crypto handshake complete for client %s", client_ip);
996 
997   // Main packet processing loop
998   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server.running)) {
999     packet_type_t packet_type;
1000     void *payload = NULL;
1001     size_t payload_size = 0;
1002 
1003     // Receive packet (blocking with system timeout)
1004     int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_size);
1005     if (result < 0) {
1006       // Check error context to distinguish timeout from actual disconnect
1007       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1008       bool has_context = HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx);
1009 
1010       // Check if this is a timeout (non-fatal) or actual disconnect (fatal)
1011       asciichat_error_t error = GET_ERRNO();
1012       if (error == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT ||
1013           (error == ERROR_NETWORK && has_context && strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "timed out") != NULL)) {
1014         // Timeout waiting for next packet - this is normal, continue waiting
1015         log_debug("Client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
1016         if (payload) {
1017           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1018         }
1019         continue;
1020       }
1021 
1022       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
1023       log_info("Client %s disconnected", client_ip);
1024       if (payload) {
1025         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1026       }
1027       break;
1028     }
1029 
1030     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1031 
1032     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
1033     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
1034       bool allowed =
1035           (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
1036 
1037       if (!allowed) {
1038         log_warn("Client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG "
1039                  "allowed",
1040                  client_ip, packet_type);
1041 
1042         // Send error response
1043         acip_transport_t *error_transport =
1044             acip_tcp_transport_create("transport_acds_error_response", client_socket, NULL);
1045         if (error_transport) {
1046           acip_send_error(error_transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1047                           "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
1048           ACDS_DESTROY_TRANSPORT(error_transport);
1049         }
1050 
1051         // Free payload and continue
1052         if (payload) {
1053           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1054         }
1055         continue;
1056       }
1057     }
1058 
1059     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
1060     // Set server context for callbacks
1061     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
1062     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
1063 
1064     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
1065         acip_handle_acds_packet(NULL, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_socket, client_ip, &callbacks);
1066 
1067     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1068       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
1069                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1070     }
1071 
1072     // Free payload (allocated by receive_packet via buffer_pool_alloc)
1073     if (payload) {
1074       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1075     }
1076   }
1077 
1078   // Cleanup
1079   tcp_server_remove_client(&server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1080   log_debug("Client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip, tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server));
1081 
1082   socket_close(client_socket);
1083   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1084 
1085   log_info("Client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1086   return NULL;
1087 }

    acip_tcp_transport_create(), acip_acds_callbacks_t::app_ctx,

    tcp_client_context_t::client_socket,
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    crypto_handshake_init(), CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_server_complete_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_server_send_parameters_socket(),
    crypto_handshake_server_start_socket(),
    private_key_t::ed25519, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, GET_ERRNO,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_complete,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_ctx, HAS_ERRNO,
    acds_server_t::identity_public,
    acds_server_t::identity_secret,
    acds_client_data_t::in_multikey_session_create,
    acds_client_data_t::joined_session, private_key_t::key,

    tcp_server::running, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,

    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_public_key, socket_close(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state,
    tcp_client_context_get_ip(), acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_add_client(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    tcp_server_reject_client(), tcp_server_remove_client(),
    private_key_t::type, public_key_t::type, and
    tcp_client_context_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_server_init().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *
  server, const acds_config_t * config)


Initialize discovery server. Loads or generates identity keys,
    opens database, creates session registry, and binds TCP socket.




Parameters


server Server structure to initialize


Config Module Configuration from command-line
  parsing


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 189 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


189                                                                                        {
190   if (!server || !config) {
191     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server or config is NULL");
192   }
193 
194   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
195   memcpy(&server->config, config, sizeof(acds_config_t));
196 
197   // Open database (SQLite as single source of truth)
198   asciichat_error_t result = database_init(config->database_path, &server->db);
199   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
200     return result;
201   }
202 
203   // Initialize rate limiter with SQLite backend
204   server->rate_limiter = rate_limiter_create_sqlite(NULL); // NULL = externally managed DB
205   if (!server->rate_limiter) {
206     database_close(server->db);
207     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create rate limiter");
208   }
209 
210   // Set the database handle for the rate limiter
211   rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(server->rate_limiter, server->db);
212 
213   // Configure TCP server
214   tcp_server_config_t tcp_config = {
215       .port = config->port,
216       .ipv4_address = (config->address[0] != '\0') ? config->address : NULL,
217       .ipv6_address = (config->address6[0] != '\0') ? config->address6 : NULL,
218       .bind_ipv4 = (config->address[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
219       .bind_ipv6 = (config->address6[0] != '\0') || (config->address[0] == '\0' && config->address6[0] == '\0'),
220       .accept_timeout_sec = 1,
221       .client_handler = acds_client_handler,
222       .user_data = server,
223   };
224 
225   // Initialize TCP server
226   result = tcp_server_init(&server->tcp_server, &tcp_config);
227   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
228     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
229     database_close(server->db);
230     return result;
231   }
232 
233   // Initialize background worker thread pool
234   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, false);
235   server->worker_pool = thread_pool_create("acds_workers");
236   if (!server->worker_pool) {
237     log_warn("Failed to create worker thread pool");
238     tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
239     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
240     database_close(server->db);
241     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create worker thread pool");
242   }
243 
244   // Spawn cleanup thread in worker pool
245   if (thread_pool_spawn(server->worker_pool, cleanup_thread_func, server, 0, "cleanup") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
246     log_warn("Failed to spawn cleanup thread (continuing without cleanup)");
247   }
248 
249   log_info("Discovery server initialized successfully");
250   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
251 }

    acds_config_t::address, acds_config_t::address6,

    acds_server_t::db, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    log_info, log_warn, acds_config_t::port,
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_create_sqlite(),
    rate_limiter_destroy(), rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(),
    SET_ERRNO, acds_server_t::shutdown,
    acds_server_t::tcp_server, tcp_server_destroy(),
    tcp_server_init(), thread_pool_create(),
    thread_pool_spawn(), and acds_server_t::worker_pool.


Referenced by acds_main().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_run (acds_server_t *
  server)


Run discovery server main loop. Accepts client connections and
    spawns handler threads. Blocks until shutdown signal received.




Parameters


server Initialized server structure


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 253 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


253                                                          {
254   if (!server) {
255     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server is NULL");
256   }
257 
258   log_info("Discovery server accepting connections on port %d", server->config.port);
259 
260   // Delegate to TCP server abstraction
261   return tcp_server_run(&server->tcp_server);
262 }






References acds_server_t::config,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_info, acds_config_t::port,
    SET_ERRNO, acds_server_t::tcp_server, and
    tcp_server_run().


Referenced by acds_main().






void
  acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t * server)


Shutdown discovery server. Closes listen socket, stops accepting
    connections, waits for handler threads to exit, closes database, and frees
    resources.




Parameters


server Server structure to clean up


Definition at line 264 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


264                                                  {
265   if (!server) {
266     return;
267   }
268 
269   // Signal shutdown to worker threads
270   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, true);
271 
272   // Shutdown TCP server (closes listen sockets, stops accept loop)
273   tcp_server_destroy(&server->tcp_server);
274 
275   // Wait for all client handler threads to exit
276   size_t remaining_clients;
277   int shutdown_attempts = 0;
278   const int max_shutdown_attempts = 100; // 10 seconds (100 * 100ms)
279 
280   while ((remaining_clients = tcp_server_get_client_count(&server->tcp_server)) > 0 &&
281          shutdown_attempts < max_shutdown_attempts) {
282     log_debug("Waiting for %zu client handler threads to exit (attempt %d/%d)", remaining_clients,
283               shutdown_attempts + 1, max_shutdown_attempts);
284     platform_sleep_ms(100);
285     shutdown_attempts++;
286   }
287 
288   if (remaining_clients > 0) {
289     log_warn("Server shutdown: %zu client handler threads still running after 10 seconds", remaining_clients);
290   } else if (shutdown_attempts > 0) {
291     log_debug("All client handler threads exited gracefully");
292   }
293 
294   // Stop and destroy worker thread pool (cleanup thread, etc.)
295   if (server->worker_pool) {
296     thread_pool_destroy(server->worker_pool);
297     server->worker_pool = NULL;
298     log_debug("Worker thread pool stopped");
299   }
300 
301   // Destroy rate limiter
302   if (server->rate_limiter) {
303     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
304     server->rate_limiter = NULL;
305   }
306 
307   // Close database
308   if (server->db) {
309     database_close(server->db);
310     server->db = NULL;
311   }
312 
313   log_info("Server shutdown complete");
314 }






References atomic_store_bool(), database_close(),
    acds_server_t::db, log_debug, log_info,
    log_warn, platform_sleep_ms(),
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_destroy(),
    acds_server_t::shutdown, acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_destroy(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    thread_pool_destroy(), and acds_server_t::worker_pool.
